thesis_status_reasoning: The architecture is experiencing compound stress across multiple critical dimensions simultaneously. AFN deviation of -$2.20 breaches the WARNING threshold and approaches the $3.00 TRIGGERED level for Kill Switch #1. The Consortium's coverage ratio of 13.8% is below the 15% TRIGGERED threshold for Kill Switch #3, with active defensive deployment already underway. Facility net outflows of $2.85B exceed the $2B/period CRITICAL threshold in the Compound Stress Matrix. Two of three critical matrix conditions are now met: AFN deviation > $2.00 and Facility net outflows > $2B/period, placing the system in CRITICAL status by design.
action_reasoning: The Compound Stress Matrix explicitly defines the current state as CRITICAL with two major indicators simultaneously breached. The Consortium has already deployed over half its reserves ($1.43B of $2.86B) yet AFN trades $2.20 below par, suggesting defensive interventions are losing effectiveness. The 13.5% ASE supply growth this period, while below the 20% WARNING level, is occurring while ASE price declines 8.2%, creating the reflexive dilution loop that critics cite.

key_signals.signal: AFN trading at $97.80, deviation of -$2.20 from par value
Stabilization Consortium coverage ratio at 13.8%, below 15% TRIGGERED threshold, actively deploying reserves
High-Yield Treasury Facility net outflows of $2.85B this period, exceeding $2B CRITICAL threshold
Posted yield of 18% vs estimated organic yield of 5.9%, creating 12.1 percentage point gap (205% of organic)
ASE supply growth of 13.5% this period while price declined 8.2%, demonstrating dilution pressure under stress
Debt-to-equity swap mechanism converting $2.6B AFN redemptions into 33.3M new ASE shares at stressed conversion rates
Institutional confidence characterized as 'deteriorating' with analyst consensus 'divided'
Facility concentration ratio of 59.4% remains below the 75% structural fragility threshold

biggest_uncertainty: Whether the Stabilization Consortium's remaining $1.43B in reserves can stabilize AFN at par before reserve exhaustion triggers the EMERGENCY scenario defined as 'Consortium collateral exhausted + swap mechanism producing > 50% ASE supply growth/period + AFN deviation > $5.00' — current trajectory suggests reserves may be insufficient given $2.85B outflows this period against $2.86B total remaining collateral.
